forecast-period.text: A high pressure system centred east of Tasmania extends a ridge over SA. A weak trough over northern SA will continue to slowly move eastwards for the remainder of today. A cold front will move across the south of SA during Saturday followed by weaker fronts near southern coasts on Sunday and Monday. Another high pressure system develops over southern WA early next week and extends a ridge into northern and western SA.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Morning fog patches. Possible morning frost patches about the South East districts. Showers about the pastoral districts and Eyre Peninsula. Isolated showers developing about western facing coastal parts during the evening. Maximum temperatures near average in the southeast and southwest, grading to below average in the north. Light winds, tending northeast to northwesterly during the day.

Isolated showers about the North East Pastoral district contracting eastwards to mostly clear by early afternoon. Showers extending across the agricultural area during the morning and early afternoon and isolated showers about the far south of the pastoral districts. Possible thunderstorms about coastal parts. Maximum temperatures near average, grading to below average about the agricultural area. Freshening north to northwesterly winds ahead of a west to southwesterly change extending across the west and agricultural areas during the day, reaching the far northeast during the evening.

Showers about the southern agricultural area, tending isolated near the west coast and about the northern agricultural area. Maximum temperatures mostly below average. Southwest to northwesterly winds easing from the west during the day.

text.p: Showers about the agricultural area, possibly extending into the south of the pastoral districts. Showers more frequent about windward coasts and ranges. Maximum temperatures near average about the agricultural area, grading to below average in the north. Northwest to southwesterly winds, fresh about southern coasts and the southeast during the morning, easing later.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Monday: Generally less than 2 mm, increasing to 2 to 5 mm east of a line roughly Coober Pedy to Ceduna, to 5 to 15 mm about the southern agricultural area and parts of Eyre Peninsula and the pastoral districts, and increasing further to 10 to 20 mm about the Mount Lofty Ranges, windward coasts and the Lower South East. Isolated falls of 15 to 30 mm are possible about windward coasts, the Mount Lofty Ranges and the Lower South East. Remaining dry in the northwest.
